_comment: Stairway arc. Same schema and mechanic as hallway-eight, different skin and story: the elevator is out, so you descend eight flights on foot. Each landing should be identical -- go back up if a landing has changed, keep descending if it hasn't.

meta.title: Stairway 8
meta.tagline: The elevator is out. Eight floors down, one landing at a time.
meta.attempt: {n} times down these stairs, and this time they let you out.
meta.attempt_first: First descent, and the stair never turned you back.

steady: A caged bulb throws steady light across the landing.
The stairwell light burns evenly overhead, as always.
The fluorescent strip on the landing glows its usual flat white.
Light falls here the same as every landing above.

flicker: The caged bulb stutters, then steadies.
The stairwell light flickers in a way it hasn't before.
Overhead, the light trips once and comes back.
dim: This landing is dimmer than the ones above it.
The light here has half given up; the corners stay dark.
A grey murk hangs over the landing.
red: The landing is lit red, an emergency bulb you don't remember.
Everything wears a low red emergency glow.
The white light is gone; only a red bulb remains.

quiet: Your footsteps echo up the shaft and fade.
The stairwell is quiet but for your own steps.
Far below, the building hums to itself.

drip: Water drips somewhere down the shaft. Tick. Tick.
A slow drip echoes up from below.
slam: A door slams far below, then silence.
Somewhere beneath you a door bangs shut.
A heavy door closes several floors down.
footsteps: Footsteps climb toward you, then stop.
You hear steps that aren't yours on the flight below.
For a moment, someone else is on the stairs.
silence: The building's hum has stopped. Dead quiet.
It is completely silent. Too silent.
Even the echo of your steps seems to vanish.

eight: A large painted 8 marks the landing wall.
The floor number, 8, is stencilled on the concrete.
'8' is painted on the wall, drips frozen beneath the strokes.
The wall reads 8, the same as the landing before.

wrong: The wall reads 7. It has always read 8.
The painted number is a 6 now, not an 8.
The floor number has changed: it says 9.
basement: The wall reads B. Basement. You are nowhere near it.
The number is gone; a painted 'B' stands in its place.
The stencil reads B, though you've barely started down.
faded: The painted number has faded to a grey ghost.
Someone has scrubbed the number half away.
The 8 on the wall is almost gone.
reversed: The 8 is painted backwards, as if seen in a mirror.
The stencilled number reads the wrong way round.

closed: The fire door to the floor is shut, its wired glass dark.
The heavy grey fire door stands closed on the landing.
The door onto the floor is closed, the way it should be.

ajar: The fire door hangs slightly ajar. A black gap, no light.
The heavy door is open a crack; it never is.
The fire door isn't quite shut this time.
propped: The fire door is propped wide open onto a dark floor.
Someone has wedged the fire door open.
The door stands fully open, held by nothing you can see.
window: The wired-glass window in the door has gone black, covered.
The little window in the fire door is blocked from the other side.
Something presses against the glass in the door.
handle: The fire door has no handle now.
Where the door handle was, only bare holes.
The handle is missing from the fire door.

out: A taped notice on the elevator reads OUT OF ORDER, USE STAIRS.
The repair notice is still there: elevator out of order.
OUT OF ORDER, says the sign taped across the elevator doors.

gone: The elevator notice is gone. Just bare metal doors.
No sign on the elevator this time.
The repair notice has been taken down.
service: The notice now reads ELEVATOR IN SERVICE.
The sign says the elevator is working again.
OUT OF ORDER has become BACK IN SERVICE.
wording: The notice reads DO NOT USE STAIRS.
The sign warns STAIRS CLOSED, it never did before.
The notice now tells you not to take the stairs at all.
arrow: The arrow on the notice points up, not down.
The sign's arrow has flipped to point back up the stairs.
The notice directs you upward now.

cold: The steel handrail runs cold and unbroken under your hand.
The painted railing is right where it always is.
Your hand finds the handrail, continuous along the flight.

gap: A section of the handrail is missing over the drop.
The railing has a gap where a length has been removed.
Part of the handrail simply isn't there.
wet: The handrail is wet, though nothing drips on it.
Your hand comes away damp from the rail.
The railing is slick under your palm.
paint: The railing is a different colour than the flights above.
Someone has repainted this stretch of handrail.
The rail's paint is green here, not grey.

present: A fire extinguisher hangs on its bracket beside the door.
The red extinguisher is in its place on the wall.
The landing's fire extinguisher sits on its hook.

gone: The fire extinguisher is gone; only the bracket remains.
No extinguisher on the wall this time.
floor: The extinguisher lies on the floor, on its side.
The fire extinguisher has been set down on the landing.
The extinguisher stands loose against the wall, off its hook.
gauge: The extinguisher's pressure gauge reads in the red.
The needle on the extinguisher sits deep in the red.
The extinguisher's gauge has dropped to empty.

clear: The flight down is clear, its steps even and dry.
The stairs descend as always, nothing on them.
The next flight drops away, clean and empty.

wet: The steps below shine wet in the light.
The next flight is slick with water.
The stairs down are wet, though you saw no spill.
missing: One of the steps in the flight below is missing.
There's a gap in the stairs where a step should be.
A tread is gone from the flight down.
up: The arrow stencilled on the steps points UP, not down.
The direction painted on the stairs has reversed.
The steps seem to lead up now, though you are going down.
debris: Something has been left on the steps below, a bag, maybe.
There's debris scattered across the next flight.
A dark shape sits partway down the flight.

below: Far below, someone else winds down the stairwell, always a flight or two ahead.
A figure descends the shaft ahead of you, keeping the same distance.
Someone is on the stairs below, going down at your pace, never closer.

facing: The figure below has stopped. It is looking up at you.
Down the shaft, the figure stands still, face turned up toward you.
The one on the stairs isn't descending now. It's watching you.
closer: The figure on the stairs is much closer than before, just a flight down.
The descending figure has closed the distance; it's nearly on your landing.
Whoever is below is only a few steps down now.
gone: The stairwell below is empty. Whoever was descending is gone.
There is no figure on the stairs this time.
The one ahead of you has vanished from the shaft.
ascending: The figure is climbing now, coming up the stairs toward you.
Footsteps rise: the figure below is ascending, not descending.
The one on the stairs has turned around and is coming up.
knows: The figure below looks up and lifts a hand, as if it has been waiting for you.
The figure mouths something up the shaft. It might be 'again.'
It beckons you down, slow and certain, as though it knows you.

steady: The shaft feels exactly as deep as it always has.
You carry the same wariness down flight after flight.
Everything feels ordinary, as far as any of this is ordinary.

deeper: The shaft feels deeper than it should, the ground floor further off.
You feel you've descended far more than eight floors.
The bottom seems to have moved further away.
forget: For a moment you can't remember how many landings you've passed.
You lose count of the flights above you, and it frightens you.
You can't recall which floor you started on.
up: For an instant you're certain you've been climbing, not descending.
A wrong feeling grips you: that down has been up all along.
You feel, briefly and coldly, that you are going the wrong way.

repeat_inspect: You check the {THING} again. You aren't sure why.
Your eyes go straight to the {THING}, the way they always seem to now.
The {THING} draws your attention once more.
long_look: You spend longer looking than usual. You don't remember deciding to stop.
You realise you've been standing on the landing. For how long?
Time slips. You were staring down the shaft, and you don't know for how long.

cutscene: Something on the landing changed, and you went down past it.
If a landing is not exactly as you remember, go back up. If it is, keep going down.
intro: You press the call button. Nothing. A notice is taped across the doors:
ELEVATOR OUT OF ORDER, USE STAIRS.
While a landing is exactly as you remember it, keep descending.
If anything has changed, the stairwell has turned you around. Climb back up.
The ground floor is the way out.
Lose count of the landings, and you'll be back at the top.
win: The last flight opens onto the lobby.
Daylight through the doors. The street. The elevator behind you, still dead.
Eight floors down, and your memory held every landing.

flashbacks: You came up in the lift once. You remember the doors closing on a face.
There was a floor you were visiting. You cannot say the number now, only that you did not want to go.
The figure below waved up at you the first time, and you told yourself it was meant for someone else.
You have read these scratches before. One of them is a tally, and it goes past eight.
You were sure the ground floor was next. You have been sure before.

exit.tempt: A landing door hangs open below, and past it the stairs seem to drop straight to the street. A shortcut down.

climb: You take the shortcut door, sure it drops you at the ground.
The number on the wall is higher than the floor you began on.
The flight down has become a flight up, and every door from here opens onto stairs that only rise.

reaction: The bar gives a finger's width and stops, a chain you cannot see holding it.
Behind it, faintly, the sound of the same stairwell going down.
herring: The paint on it is tacky, freshly done, though the sign beside it is yellow with age.
A cold draft comes up through the gap, carrying nothing you can name.
fold: It swings open onto a landing you passed three floors ago. The count is gone. You are at the top again.

reaction: It is colder than the air, and it hums faintly, the way a rail does when someone far below holds it too.
Your hand leaves no print on it, though it should.
herring: Cold, where you left it warm a landing ago. It means nothing.
It is a shade of green you do not remember it being. The light down here is bad.
fold: You follow it down with your eyes, and your feet follow your eyes, and you are climbing again, at the start.

reaction: LIFT OUT OF ORDER, USE STAIRS. Someone has underlined USE, twice, hard.
Below the print, in pencil, a hand has written a number and crossed it out.
herring: The tape holding it is fresh, still shiny. Notices like this are always being replaced.
The paper is warm, as if just off a machine. Buildings are strange at night.
fold: You read to the bottom and the bottom says START, and it is.

reaction: Your voice comes back a beat late, in your own tone.
Far below, a face tips up toward the sound, or the shadow of one.
herring: The coat down there is the colour of yours. Most coats are.
It is a landing nearer than when you called. You have been walking, after all.
fold: It climbs to meet you, unhurried, and when it reaches your landing you are somehow back at the top, alone.
